No tengo mucho para decir, es agradable, buena atención, gente amable!! Quizá deberían ser más ágiles, la gente que vacaciona esta apurada y no queremos perder una hora cargando nafta!!

(Translated by Google)
I don't have much to say, it's nice, good service, friendly people! Maybe they should be more efficient; vacationers are in a hurry and we don't want to waste an hour filling up with gas!

reviews.comment:
No lleguen alrededor de las 14, con motivo del "cambio de turno" la atención se demoró media hora. Es decir, estuvimos haciendo cola media hora, había dos playeros y cuando llegamos al surtidor, uno de los playeros se fue...volvió después de un rato y nos preguntó cómo íbamos a pagar. Nuestra intención era pagar con débito, pero nos dijo que "por el cambio de turno" íbamos a tener que esperar para pagar con débito. (El sistema cambia de turno también?) Después de haber esperado media hora, nos pareció simplemente una falta de respeto que no aceptaran débito. Por suerte, teníamos efectivo suficiente, pero pagar nafta no es barato y hace falta cierta cantidad de efectivo, que no siempre uno lleva encima. Y en ningún momento se disculparon, ni por la espera, ni por la "imposibilidad" de cobrarnos con débito. Evidentemente, al ser una de las dos estaciones de San Martín de los Andes, tienen una clientela asegurada y no la cuidan en lo más mínimo

(Translated by Google)
Don't arrive around 2 PM, because due to a "shift change," service was delayed by half an hour. We waited in line for half an hour; there were two attendants, and when we got to the pump, one of them left. He came back after a while and asked us how we were going to pay. We intended to pay by debit card, but he told us that "because of the shift change," we would have to wait to pay with debit. (Does the system change shifts too?) After waiting half an hour, we simply felt it was disrespectful that they wouldn't accept debit. Luckily, we had enough cash, but gas isn't cheap, and you need a certain amount of cash, which you don't always carry with you. And at no point did they apologize, neither for the wait nor for the "impossibility" of accepting debit. Evidently, being one of the two gas stations in San Martín de los Andes, they have a guaranteed clientele and don't take care of them in the slightest.
reviews.name: accounts/109853746269412609224/locations/13749144040617922376/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSURzck9Qei1RRRAB
